title: Chamisme angustifolia
taxonomy: Chamisme angustifolia is classified within the plant family Lamiaceae and is commonly known as the Mexican oregano or narrow-leaf Mexican oregano. This plant is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. It belongs to the genus Lippia. Lippia angustifolia is also another name for this plant, which is often used interchangeably with Chamisme angustifolia. This classification indicates the plant's botanical identity and relationship to other species within the Lamiaceae family.

intro:
There is not enough information available about the specific plant species Chamisme angustifolia. However, I can provide information on a similar plant, Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla), as it is often referred to by the name Chamisme in some sources. 

Chamomile is an annual herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family. The health benefits of chamomile include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and insomnia, and soothe digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of chamomile include apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made with chamomile include tea, tinctures, and topical creams, which are often used to treat skin irritations and promote relaxation.
